title: Did you think about loading the data

messages.author: Skinkie
messages.text: For the region in The Netherlands were I am active we can easily query such. There are enough views on the data that allow reconstruction. At the point people are trying all kind of crazy things like reducing hops, adding regions, etc. to mitigate what they observe as 'bad mesh behavior.' When looking at the topology of the network, and routing, my hunch is there are bugs in the mesh code, virtualizing this and simulating those effects should like a better way to solve issues.
messages.content_html: For the region in The Netherlands were I am active we can easily query such. There are enough views on the data that allow reconstruction. At the point people are trying all kind of crazy things like reducing hops, adding regions, etc. to mitigate what they observe as 'bad mesh behavior.' When looking at the topology of the network, and routing, my hunch is there are bugs in the mesh code, virtualizing this and simulating those effects should like a better way to solve issues.

messages.author: stachumann
messages.text: Ha, that was the reason I started that exercise. I was surprised how little private messages is delivered vs how public channel behaves. Now I'm running simulations using auto-delay adjustments (have quite promising results already), but need a view from outside. Most important - build realistic topology map. Then we can inject regions and all kind of simulated communication. You can try from repository - edit 'prepare_gdansk_test.py' to cover your area - to built a basic simulation, then you can view it using ./toosl/run_sim.py - of course on linux machine
messages.content_html: Ha, that was the reason I started that exercise. I was surprised how little private messages is delivered vs how public channel behaves. Now I'm running simulations using auto-delay adjustments (have quite promising results already), but need a view from outside. Most important - build realistic topology map. Then we can inject regions and all kind of simulated communication. You can try from repository - edit 'prepare_gdansk_test.py' to cover your area - to built a basic simulation, then you can view it using ./toosl/run_sim.py - of course on linux machine

messages.author: Skinkie
messages.text: Would it help if you can fetch telemetry from repeaters. Or do you need all those repeaters to pushing data?
messages.content_html: Would it help if you can fetch telemetry from repeaters. Or do you need all those repeaters to pushing data?

messages.author: paul / BostonMesh
messages.text: I’m hoping to get some time to setup the simulation based on our topography to test the auto-delay firmware. We’ve merged it with the powersavings firmware and have a heltec V4 binary that a number of folks are running. I’ve adjusted all the of the repeaters near my home repeater and went from 30% collisions down to 10%.
messages.content_html: I’m hoping to get some time to setup the simulation based on our topography to test the auto-delay firmware. We’ve merged it with the powersavings firmware and have a heltec V4 binary that a number of folks are running. I’ve adjusted all the of the repeaters near my home repeater and went from 30% collisions down to 10%.

messages.author: stachumann
messages.text: Downloading neighbor list with connection quality would help to make a better topology for simulation, that's for sure, for aut-delays - I'll post my results (over 12h of simulation for different scenarios... - where one take split second) on github discussion thread
messages.content_html: Downloading neighbor list with connection quality would help to make a better topology for simulation, that's for sure, for aut-delays - I'll post my results (over 12h of simulation for different scenarios... - where one take split second) on github discussion thread

messages.author: Skinkie
messages.text: I have the following question that I want to have a validation of. Considering perfect propagation between far nodes A -> B -> E -> F. Still the path for a direct message is via A -> B -> C -> D -> E -> F. Considering that repeater E receives the message via the direct path. Is this message repeated or, ignored?
messages.content_html: I have the following question that I want to have a validation of. Considering perfect propagation between far nodes A -> B -> E -> F. Still the path for a direct message is via A -> B -> C -> D -> E -> F. Considering that repeater E receives the message via the direct path. Is this message repeated or, ignored?

messages.author: stachumann
messages.text: This is the test file created for your case: { "_name": "t31_direct_path_overhear", "_desc": "Explores flood path selection and direct-route overhear behavior. Topology: alice-B-C-D-E-frank chain (strong links, SNR 10) plus a weaker B-E shortcut (SNR 3, bidirectional). Question: which path does the flood learn, and how do direct messages behave when intermediate repeaters can overhear transmissions not addressed to them?", "simulation": { "duration_ms": 180000, "step_ms": 4, "warmup_ms": 5000, "hot_start": true, "seed": 42 }, "nodes": [ {"name": "alice", "role": "companion"}, {"name": "B", "role": "repeater"}, {"name": "C", "role": "repeater"}, {"name": "D", "role": "repeater"}, {"name": "E", "role": "repeater"}, {"name": "frank", "role": "companion"} ], "topology": { "links": [ {"from": "alice", "to": "B", "snr": 10, "rssi": -70}, {"from": "B", "to": "C", "snr": 10, "rssi": -70}, {"from": "C", "to": "D", "snr": 10, "rssi": -70}, {"from": "D", "to": "E", "snr": 10, "rssi": -70}, {"from": "E", "to": "frank", "snr": 10, "rssi": -70}, {"from": "B", "to": "E", "snr": 3, "rssi": -90} ] }, "commands": [ {"at_ms": 6000, "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank flood_msg"}, {"at_ms": 60000, "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank direct_msg1"}, {"at_ms": 110000, "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank direct_msg2"} ], "expect": [ {"type": "cmd_reply_contains", "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank flood_msg", "value": "sent"}, {"type": "cmd_reply_contains", "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank direct_msg1", "value": "sent"}, {"type": "cmd_reply_contains", "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank direct_msg2", "value": "sent"} ] } I'm attaching visualizations (which allow also path inspection) - this is with default meshcore, Another scenario can be with tx-delay...
messages.content_html: This is the test file created for your case: { "_name": "t31_direct_path_overhear", "_desc": "Explores flood path selection and direct-route overhear behavior. Topology: alice-B-C-D-E-frank chain (strong links, SNR 10) plus a weaker B-E shortcut (SNR 3, bidirectional). Question: which path does the flood learn, and how do direct messages behave when intermediate repeaters can overhear transmissions not addressed to them?", "simulation": { "duration_ms": 180000, "step_ms": 4, "warmup_ms": 5000, "hot_start": true, "seed": 42 }, "nodes": [ {"name": "alice", "role": "companion"}, {"name": "B", "role": "repeater"}, {"name": "C", "role": "repeater"}, {"name": "D", "role": "repeater"}, {"name": "E", "role": "repeater"}, {"name": "frank", "role": "companion"} ], "topology": { "links": [ {"from": "alice", "to": "B", "snr": 10, "rssi": -70}, {"from": "B", "to": "C", "snr": 10, "rssi": -70}, {"from": "C", "to": "D", "snr": 10, "rssi": -70}, {"from": "D", "to": "E", "snr": 10, "rssi": -70}, {"from": "E", "to": "frank", "snr": 10, "rssi": -70}, {"from": "B", "to": "E", "snr": 3, "rssi": -90} ] }, "commands": [ {"at_ms": 6000, "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank flood_msg"}, {"at_ms": 60000, "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank direct_msg1"}, {"at_ms": 110000, "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank direct_msg2"} ], "expect": [ {"type": "cmd_reply_contains", "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank flood_msg", "value": "sent"}, {"type": "cmd_reply_contains", "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank direct_msg1", "value": "sent"}, {"type": "cmd_reply_contains", "node": "alice", "command": "msg frank direct_msg2", "value": "sent"} ] } I'm attaching visualizations (which allow also path inspection) - this is with default meshcore, Another scenario can be with tx-delay...

messages.author: stachumann
messages.text: Later will post variants with delays, it is interesting to run simulations and see how it behaves...
messages.content_html: Later will post variants with delays, it is interesting to run simulations and see how it behaves...

messages.author: stachumann
messages.text: Fixed installation instructions, now should be easy to follow. I do suggest to install and test, it is interesting to see, how messages spread, where collision occurs, what reach what. I can post more samples here.

messages.text: I'll introduce your work to the dutch community

messages.author: Skinkie
stachumann
messages.text: As said - just edit 'prepare_gdansk...' - line with python3 -m topology_generator \ --region 53.7,17.3,54.8,19.5 \ put there your coordinates to cover test area
messages.content_html: As said - just edit 'prepare_gdansk...' - line with python3 -m topology_generator \ --region 53.7,17.3,54.8,19.5 \ put there your coordinates to cover test area
